urn:contentItem:6JH1-B483-RVD8-2234-00000-00CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING BALFOUR BEATTY PLC AND PEERS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:41:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING BALFOUR BEATTY PLC AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC

PermID4296619307Websitehttp://www.clarkconstruction.comIndustryConstructionAddress18201 Von Karman Avenue Suite 800 Irvine, California 92612

ACTIVITIES:

Clark Construction Group, LLC, founded in 1906, is a leading general contractor headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland. With over 4,000 employees across 20+ offices, it serves commercial, infrastructure, and institutional sectors, delivering projects like stadiums, hospitals, and transit systems. Generating over $5 billion annually, Clark is known for its use of Building Information Modeling (BIM) and sustainable practices. In 2024, it completed major infrastructure projects, emphasizing green construction. Its culture prioritizes safety, diversity, and employee development, earning industry accolades. Competing with Turner Construction, Clark stands out for its large-scale project expertise and client partnerships. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy construction and digital tools for project management.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP APPOINTS CARLY REINER CACERES AS NATIONAL CAMPUS RECRUITING HR DIRECTOR

Clark Construction Group appointed Carly Reiner Caceres as Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting, effective Apr. 29, 2026, with a mandate to advance national campus recruiting initiatives and strengthen early-career talent pipelines for its broad portfolio of projects.Caceres arrives from Proper, where she served as Vice President of People Operations, shaping HR strategy and operations in a hybrid workplace. Her background also includes leadership roles at MILLER as Director of Internal Operations and more than nine years at Accenture as Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Her prior experience at Grant Thornton LLP overseeing campus recruiting strategies further reinforces her capability to build robust internship programs and solid student engagement initiatives.Clark Construction Group, established in 1906, has a long track record across education and infrastructure markets. The company says Caceres will bring strategic HR expertise to attract top graduates and mentor the next generation of construction professionals.Her early career featured internships at Charleston Magazine and the Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum, rounding out a diverse background in communications and external relations that will help guide talent development efforts.

+ RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL AWARDS MULTI-TRADES FRAMEWORK CONTRACT TO CLARK CONTRACTS FOR DOMESTIC HOUSING WORKS

Clark Contracts has been appointed to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades contractors framework to improve delivery of repair, maintenance and refurbishment services for domestic properties across the authority. The appointment was announced on 11th February 2026 and the framework runs through 2027.The framework covers a broad range of multi-trade works, enabling the council to manage both responsive and planned maintenance more effectively across its housing stock and ensuring residents benefit from a faster, more reliable service.Clark Contracts is a well-established public-sector supplier, with current agreements including Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework. The company also participates in the Crown Commercial Service’s Construction Framework and several Scottish Procurement Alliance frameworks.Gordon Cunningham, managing director at Clark Contracts, said the appointment builds on a track record of more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over the past three years and demonstrates the firm’s commitment to serving local communities.The deal supports Clark Contracts’ growing public-sector footprint as it works with councils and institutions such as the University of Glasgow and Glasgow City Council to deliver essential housing services.

+ CDC APPROVES AMENDMENT FOR ₱4-B THE VILLAGES DEVELOPMENT, PKR JOINS CLARK GROWTH DRIVE

Clark Freeport is set to host a ₱4-billion mixed-use residential and commercial development in The Villages after Clark Development Corp. approved an amendment to PKR Corp.'s lease on December 13, 2025. The revised lease provides PKR with a 30-month window to complete construction and includes safeguards aimed at ensuring on-time delivery. Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera, CDC president and CEO, noted that the amendment reinforces investor confidence and the efficient execution of projects, while keeping The Villages as a community hub where residents can live and participate. PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung thanked CDC for the partnership and said the company will deliver the Clark project, with PKR operating under Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean firm known for safety, technology, and workplace practices. The initiative fits CDC's broader push to accelerate investments, promote well-planned communities, and establish a business-friendly environment that appeals to both global and local investors.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BALFOUR BEATTY [3 News That Matters]

+ TRENT JOHNSON NAMED VICE PRESIDENT OF AVIATION OPERATIONS FOR BALFOUR BEATTY IN THE CAROLINAS

Balfour Beatty promoted Trent Johnson to Vice President of operations for the Carolinas, effective Apr. 1, 2026. In the newly expanded role, he will lead and grow the company's aviation practice across North and South Carolina, leveraging nearly two decades in the construction industry. Johnson brings 12-and-a-half years with Balfour Beatty and a total of 19 years in the field. He has delivered 22 aviation projects, including targeted renovations and multi-phase programmes, with notable work at Raleigh-Durham International Airport and Albert J. Ellis Airport, helping to solidify the company's aviation footprint in the region. In the new role, he will oversee the aviation segment across the Carolinas, continuing to support existing clients while pursuing opportunities at additional regional and international airports. His focus will include guiding project teams through active airport environments, prioritising safety, operational continuity, and stakeholder coordination. Jeff Sandeen, President of Balfour Beatty in the Carolinas, lauded Johnson for building a strong reputation as a trusted leader and client partner, and said his leadership will drive the next phase of growth for the company's aviation portfolio. Johnson holds a degree in Construction Engineering Management from North Carolina State University and has been active in the community, notably with the Make-A-Wish Foundation.

+ MAINMARK UK SECURES ROLE ON BALFOUR BEATTY CP7 FRAMEWORK TO SUPPORT NETWORK RAIL

Mainmark UK has been awarded a place on the Balfour Beatty plc CP7 Framework, positioning the geotechnical contractor to support Network Rail’s CP7 investment programme running from 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2029, with an overall envelope of around £43-45 billion across Great Britain.CP7 is designed to maintain, renew, and modernise the railway, delivering a safer, more reliable, and more sustainable network. Investment priorities include infrastructure maintenance and renewals, digital signalling and technology, safety improvements, and initiatives to improve both freight and passenger performance.Within the CP7 Framework, contractors will undertake a mix of activities such as low complexity emergency callouts, planned preventative maintenance, and small scale civil engineering schemes. Mainmark UK will take on geotechnical response works, bridge repairs, structural integrity projects, platform refurbishments, and drainage clearance, among other tasks.Tom Kavanagh, General Manager at Mainmark UK, commented, "We’re looking forward to collaborating with Balfour Beatty to help deliver critical infrastructure works across the network during the CP7 period. Being selected to support Balfour Beatty on this framework is a fantastic opportunity for our team and aligns perfectly with our growth plans."Mainmark is renowned for its Teretek resin injection technology, which stabilises weak ground and fills subsurface voids with minimal disruption. As heavier freight loads and severe weather stress railway assets, Teretek helps preserve structural integrity and asset resilience. The CP7 appointment positions Mainmark to contribute to a more resilient UK rail network.

+ BALFOUR BEATTY POSTS 9% EPS RISE AS UK-US PROJECTS SUPPORT REVENUE GROWTH

Balfour Beatty PLC posted strong growth, powered by its construction arm, notably power-transmission work. The group’s market value has climbed about 66% over the past year as it rides a record order book, while underlying earnings per share rose 9% to 47.6 pence. On 11 March 2026, the group said top-line growth was supported by UK demand for power transmission and a surge in US building activity. Operating profits in the support services segment jumped a third to £122m, and the underlying margin rose 80 basis points to 8.5%. The UK construction business also exceeded its margin targets ahead of schedule. In the US, rising building volumes helped the group grow, though it faced cost overruns on a Civils project. The company maintains a dual-market strategy designed to sustain profitability amid these challenges. Analysts remain upbeat about the outlook, noting the large and expanding order book amid ongoing infrastructure investment. Balfour Beatty aims to improve margins and deliver projects on time, reinforcing its position in both the UK and US markets. Looking ahead, the company sees opportunity in expanding power grids and broader construction demand, while remaining mindful of rising costs and project-management challenges that could affect near-term performance.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION TO GROW INNSBROOK FOOTPRINT WITH 9,100-SF EXPANSION BACKED BY HENRICO COUNTY

DPR Construction announced a 9,100-square-foot expansion of its Innsbrook office, supported by Henrico County incentives to attract and retain businesses. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The expansion aims to accommodate a growing workforce and strengthen operational capabilities as part of the company’s regional growth strategy.The project will bring together teams and resources, promoting collaboration and innovation across the organization.Sustainable design features and modern office concepts will be integrated, aligning with DPR’s leadership in sustainable construction.Completion is expected within the next year, underscoring the company’s commitment to the region and its ongoing community initiatives.

+ JASON PAGE APPOINTED HEAD OF DPR CONSTRUCTION'S NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP

The Special Services Group handles a broad range of work, from targeted improvements like parking garage stair installations to expansive interior buildouts for new office and entertainment spaces, and Page's leadership is expected to expand the group's capabilities to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs in Nashville. Beyond project leadership, Page mentors and coaches teams, sharing best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work and fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation as DPR expands its Nashville footprint.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION SECURES GREAT PLACE TO WORK STATUS AS STAFF REPORT HIGH ENGAGEMENT

DPR Construction has secured Great Place To Work Certification, confirming that 97 percent of its employees say it is a great place to work, a figure about 40 points higher than the national average. The award, presented on 31st March 2026, signals a culture rooted in pride, belonging and opportunity across the firm’s projects.CEO George Pfeffer noted the certification reflects sentiments across the organization. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said. The company emphasizes trust, support, and growth to help employees perform at their best.DPR’s operating model empowers staff to make decisions and share information openly, encouraging initiative and cross functional collaboration on complex tasks, new roles, and ideas beyond job descriptions. A strong emphasis on learning and development supports clear career paths aligned with individual aspirations.Great Place To Work® research aligns with these findings, highlighting a sense of pride and belonging among workers. The certification has helped DPR strengthen its reputation as an employer of choice, while external accolades from U.S. News & World Report and Newsweek have recognized DPR in more than 15 U.S. cities over the last year.Founded in 1990, DPR has evolved into a multi billion dollar contractor known for complex and sustainable projects across sectors. Its innovation posture—through virtual design and construction and self performing craft—helps explain its roughly 11,000 person workforce worldwide. More information about DPR’s culture and career opportunities is available on its official website.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ TURNER CELEBRATES TOPPING OUT OF 72-MW ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER IN FREDERICK

Turner Construction Company marked the topping out of the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, signifying the completion of the structure for the 72-megawatt facility. The milestone was achieved on the 28th of April 2026, less than five months after ground had been broken. To date, the project has logged more than 80,000 craft hours, with roughly 345 skilled workers contributing to progress. The 450,000-square-foot center sits on a 75-acre campus and features a two-story structural steel design, an insulated precast exterior system, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling solution that combines liquid and air-cooled technologies to optimize performance. Turner's leadership emphasized that topping out represents more than erecting the frame, underscoring the strength of the partnership with Aligned and the team’s commitment to safe, efficient delivery. As the project moves into interior fit-out and operational readiness, stakeholders will watch closely, anticipating the facility’s role in supporting the region’s digital infrastructure.

+ GEISINGER WYOMING VALLEY EXPANSION MOVES TO VERTICAL CONSTRUCTION AS TURNER BREAKS GROUND

WILKES-BARRE, PA — Turner Construction has advanced to vertical construction on Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center's $900 million expansion, moving forward after years of planning and foundation work. Steel now rises on an 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower that will lift beds from 356 to 500 and expand operating rooms from 12 to 18. Officials say the addition will enlarge the emergency department and ensure all patient rooms are private, with completion expected in the first quarter of 2028. The date of this announcement is 28th April 2026.David Kaminski, Turner’s Vice President and Senior General Manager, described the milestone as a key step toward broader health care access for families in northeastern Pennsylvania and a chance to partner with Geisinger on a project that strengthens the community for decades. The project is projected to create as many as 3,800 construction jobs and generate about $1 billion in local economic impact. Onsite employment is expected to rise from 150 workers to as many as 450 during peak activity, with Geisinger planning to hire an additional 400 staff after opening.Geisinger Wyoming Valley's campus opened in 1981 and has since expanded to meet rising demand for medical services. The new tower will bring advanced capabilities comparable to those typically found in major academic medical centers serving large metropolitan areas.

+ JMA VENTURES NAMES TURNER CONSTRUCTION LEAD ON WESTCOURT ORLANDO DEVELOPMENT

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, a development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a milestone in the project’s progression. The decision to partner with Turner stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, said that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, including coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches on Florida’s Space Coast. He recalled an early meeting in which he told the team, "If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us." Turner has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, handling pre-construction with JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Early site activities such as utility mapping and surveying are underway to ensure a smooth transition into the next phases. The Westcourt project is valued at approximately $500 million and will include a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center, with peak on-site employment around 800 workers.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN LAUNCHES FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ACCELERATE CONSTRUCTION MANUFACTURING

JE Dunn Construction has launched Form Off-Site Solutions, its off-site manufacturing initiative focused on Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex construction components. The program, announced with a launch date of 6th April 2026, builds on the company’s century-long Kansas City heritage while extending capabilities nationwide.Form Off-Site Solutions structures its offerings into four product segments: metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies, designed to meet the varied needs of owners, general contractors, trade partners, and original equipment manufacturers.The end-to-end service model covers estimating, design, manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, all aimed at improving installation efficiency, safety, and quality, while providing greater cost certainty and helping shorten project timelines amid labor shortages.Off-Site Manufacturing Director Nick Effenheim emphasizes collaboration: "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and focus on getting their ideas, concepts, and design into a manufactured environment is something that sets us apart."JE Dunn President and CEO Gordon Lansford says the Form launch marks a major step in the firm’s ongoing push to drive innovation for clients as Form’s production facilities in Kansas City and Nashville position the unit to serve a national client base.

+ STEEL BEAM PLACED AS NORTHLAND CENTER REACHES TOPPING-OUT MILESTONE

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — The topping-out of the Northland Workforce Development Center was celebrated on 19th March 2026, as JE Dunn Construction placed the final structural steel beam for the 145,000-square-foot, two-story facility.Before the beam was hoisted into place, a beam-signing ceremony brought together the Platte County R-3 School District, partner districts, project stakeholders, and community members who signed the beam bearing project and partner logos. Brad Hokanson, Project Executive at JE Dunn Construction, said, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The center is being built to house programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services, expanding capacity for Northland Career Center and partner institutions while serving both high school students and adult learners seeking credentials. Planned spaces include flexible labs, classrooms, hands-on training environments, and technology-rich areas that reflect real-world workplaces.Located in the Northland region of Kansas City, the project is progressing on schedule, with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening expected after the milestone. Executive Director Brian Noller of Platte County School District noted that state, local government, business, investor, and community foundation support have enabled a long-term investment in students and the region.JE Dunn is serving as construction manager, working in partnership with the school district and Clark & Enersen, with a focus on safety, schedule adherence, and coordination while maintaining open communication with stakeholders and minimizing disruption to nearby facilities.

+ JE DUNN REINSTATES CHRIS CHELETTE AS SENIOR VP, HEALTHCARE, TO EXPAND REGIONAL PRESENCE

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025. During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region. In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction. Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively. As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: Earthjustice: Court Hearing - Oregon 's Largest Gas Utility Seeks to Undo Energy Victory Won by Public Interest Groups to Help Lower Rising Energy Costs

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGH-F1M3-S2B6-Y444-00000-00Earthjustice: Court Hearing - Oregon's Largest Gas Utility Seeks to Undo Energy Victory Won by Public Interest Groups to Help Lower Rising Energy Costs2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:41:31ZLexisNexisEarthjustice: Court Hearing - Oregon's Largest Gas Utility Seeks to Undo Energy Victory Won by Public Interest Groups to Help Lower Rising Energy CostsTargeted News ServiceSAN FRANCISCO, California

Earthjustice issued the following news release:

* * *

Court Hearing: Oregon's Largest Gas Utility Seeks to Undo Energy Victory Won by Public Interest Groups to Help Lower Rising Energy Costs

NW Natural is seeking to overturn the groups' victory, phasing out gas pipeline subsidies that the company passes on to customers, resulting in higher energy costs

*

Salem, OR -- Two years ago, climate, justice, and consumer advocacy groups won a significant victory in a utility rate case against Oregon's largest gas utility, NW Natural, to help provide relief from skyrocketing energy bills. Oregon utility regulators sided with the groups after a five-year fight, ordering NW Natural to phase out gas pipeline subsidies by November 2027 and disallowing $13.7 million in overspending on pipes.

The victory would help contain rising costs by shaving millions of dollars off future rate hikes - and help the Pacific Northwest meet its goals to reduce carbon emissions to help mitigate climate change.

The gas pipeline subsidies, known as line extension allowances, are a tool utilities use to subsidize the cost of new gas lines and hookups, encouraging new homes and businesses to be built with gas. These subsidies allow the utility to expand its gas infrastructure without charging new customers the true costs. Instead, existing customers pay for the expense of locking Oregon communities further into fossil fuel use.

NW Natural appealed this victory, and next week, the Oregon Court of Appeals will hear arguments from both parties during a court hearing. The Oregon Department of Justice will defend the Oregon Public Utility Commission's order on behalf of itself and the public interest groups that intervened in the utility rate case.

In addition to the Citizens' Utility Board, representing residential customers, the groups that won the victory in 2024 are the Coalition of Communities of Color, Climate Solutions, Verde, Columbia Riverkeeper, Oregon Environmental Council, Community Energy Project, and Sierra Club; the groups are represented by Earthjustice and the Green Energy Institute at Lewis & Clark Law School.

How to listen in

The public may tune in to the oral argument at 9 a.m. on Wednesday, May 6.

* View the court hearing via livestream (https://oregoncourts.mediasite.com/mediasite/Channel/default/browse/null/most-recent/null/0/35f7f5a59c91443889c04c11fc94f7eb14).

* Attend in person at the Oregon Court of Appeals, 1163 State Street NE, Salem, Oregon 97301

Statements from the public interest groups and their attorneys:

"NW Natural is challenging a decision that will save Oregonians millions of dollars by limiting unnecessary gas system expansion, while simultaneously suing to roll back Oregon's Climate Protection Program- one of the state's most important tools for protecting households and businesses from rising energy cost risk," said Nora Apter, Oregon Director at Climate Solutions. "If the company were truly focused on ratepayer interests and affordability, it would not be fighting the very policies designed to do exactly that."

"A line extension allowance is a subsidy to expand fossil fuel infrastructure paid for by current gas utility customers, plain and simple," said Dylan Plummer, Campaign Strategist with Sierra Club's Clean Heat Campaign. "Recognizing its legal obligations to support our state to meet its climate targets, the Oregon Public Utility Commission made the common-sense decision to phase out these backward subsidies, joining numerous Commissions across the country doing the same. Northwest Natural's appeal seeks to protect fossil fuel interests while keeping Oregon ratepayers on the hook to pay for more polluting gas pipelines."

"Eliminating subsidies for expanding the gas system was a big win for Oregonians," said Bob Jenks, Executive Director of Oregon Citizens' Utility Board. "Our analysis showed that these subsidies benefit shareholders but are harmful to existing customers. As Oregon commits to reducing climate pollution, NW Natural's expansion of the gas system places a real risk on Oregon households."

"The Commission's order we're defending in this appeal reflected serious concern about the utility's spending," said Earthjustice attorney Jaimini Parekh. "Line extension allowances are perverse incentives that favor the fossil fuel industry by growing our demand for gas at the expense of all of us who pay energy bills. We are fighting to remove these harmful subsidies and curb other wasteful spending by NW Natural that customers have been paying for with skyrocketing energy bills."

Background

NW Natural is Oregon's largest gas utility, providing more than 2.5 million people in Oregon and Southwest Washington with methane gas, a powerful greenhouse gas that traps heat in the atmosphere and is a primary contributor to human-caused climate change.

The utility has actively fought against electrification efforts in the Pacific Northwest, and instead pushed for costly gas investments and promoted its pro-gas agenda through misleading propaganda. It previously challenged Oregon's Climate Protection Program in court. This is Oregon's climate policy that regulates climate pollution from the state's largest fossil fuel emitters, including gas utilities. NW Natural won this legal challenge on a procedural technicality, prompting new rulemaking to fix the error and restore the program. It is now challenging the Climate Protection Program in court again.

The public interest groups won an earlier victory in 2022 against NW Natural when the Oregon Public Utility Commission ruled that Oregon's largest gas utility, NW Natural, could no longer charge its customers to target schoolchildren with pro-gas propaganda booklets. The order also prohibited NW Natural from lobbying local governments to prevent climate action and required an increase in energy-efficiency measures.

Oregon Citizens' Utility Board, alongside the groups represented by Earthjustice and the Green Energy Institute, also argued for and won a reduction in subsidies for new gas hookups, from $2,875 to $1380 between November 2022 and November 2024. That order in 2022 set up a system for declining the amount over three years permitted for line extension allowances, but it did not phase them out entirely.

In the intervening years, CUB found that NW Natural was overspending despite the cap. The most expensive connection NW Natural covered was $86,553 for a single home in 2021, when the cap was $2,875. This discovery of overspending led to the 2024 ruling that the gas utility must remove $13.7 million from customer rates. The complete phase out of these subsidies was secured in the victorious 2024 order.

* * *

About Earthjustice

Earthjustice is the premier nonprofit environmental law organization. We wield the power of law and the strength of partnership to protect people's health, to preserve magnificent places and wildlife, to advance clean energy, and to combat climate change. We are here because the earth needs a good lawyer.

* * *

[Category: Environment]

Contact: Elizabeth Manning, Earthjustice, emanning@earthjustice.org

Juan M. Munoz Jimenez Climate Solutions, juan.munoz@climatesolutions.org

value.Title: Robo.Ai CEO Issues Letter To Investors On 2025 Financial Results: Company Reaching Inflection Point With Positive Cash Flow And Business Order Delivery

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JG9-4YP3-S2GT-830N-00000-00Robo.Ai CEO Issues Letter To Investors On 2025 Financial Results: Company Reaching Inflection Point With Positive Cash Flow And Business Order Delivery2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:41:31ZLexisNexisRobo.Ai CEO Issues Letter To Investors On 2025 Financial Results: Company Reaching Inflection Point With Positive Cash Flow And Business Order Delivery

DUBAI, UAE, May 1, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Robo Inc. (NASDAQ: AIIO ) recently filed its annual financial report for the year 2025. Benjamin Zhai, the Chief Executive Officer, issued a letter to investors and strategic partners detailing the company's strategic reorganization and operational progress since the new management team assumed their roles in May 2025.


In the AI software segment, the company is advancing its partnership with DaBoss, a Silicon Valley-based AI model data service provider. Operations for intelligent data training and collection are underway across multiple locations, aiming to deliver tens of thousands of hours of real-world interaction data and systematically realize commercial value. Additionally, Robo is exploring strategic partnership opportunities with leading technology companies in the AI data application sector to address technical challenges and costs associated with large-scale data storage, transmission, and processing.
Regarding smart hardware, the company provided an operational update on April 10, 2026, confirming the production and initial delivery of commercial validation vehicles from its subsidiary, Robus. The initial batch of vehicles has passed client acceptance, and a second batch was successfully delivered and accepted in the South Asian market this week. Management views the initial fulfillment of these orders as validation of the commercial feasibility of its smart hardware strategy, and the company plans to continue allocating resources to meet global market demand.
The company continues to invest in its "Machine Economy" strategy, which seeks to integrate artificial intelligence, robotics, and blockchain technology with physical assets in the real world. Following his participation in the Web3 Festival in Hong Kong, the CEO characterized this technological convergence as an emerging "R2R" (Robot World to Real World) business model, drawing parallels to the booming of "O2O" business model (online-to-offline integration) around 10 years ago. As part of this ecosystem, Arkreen, a Singaporean blockchain company within Robo's investment portfolio, deployed a distributed solar project named eCandle in Africa this month. This initiative establishes an innovative digital payment infrastructure for the machine economy while successfully integrating green energy, digital assets, and social responsibilities.
Addressing the 2025 financial results, management noted that the reported net loss was primarily driven by non-cash items and legacy matters. These included considerable amount of non-cash expenses related to shares issuance as well as impairment and liability provisions. Despite these accounting impacts, overall net cash flow turned positive as a result of cost controls and operational optimizations implemented in recent quarters, and the management considers this a critical positive inflection point. As a result of recent divestiture of a specific Cayman-based subsidiary, it is expected to yield a potential asset disposal gain of approximately $60 million.
As a potential strategic investment target for Robo, Shanghai Jidu Auto formally entered the judicial restructuring phase on April 29, 2026, following a pre-restructuring process initiated last year. Robo's potential investment is intended to secure quality technology assets and strengthen its foundational capabilities in the smart mobility sector.
Looking ahead to 2026, management acknowledges macroeconomic challenges stemming from geopolitical conditions in the Middle East, but notes that the region's increasing demand for independent supply chains, international capital, and technology presents ongoing operational opportunities that align with Robo's globalized resources.
Benjamin reiterates the management team's commitment to and confidence in the transformation of Robo.
About Robo Inc.
Robo Inc. (NASDAQ: AIIO ) is a technology company dedicated to building a leading global artificial intelligence machine economy platform. Its mission is to integrate "AI Software, Intelligent Hardware, and Smart Assets" to construct a unified AI operating system and an ecosystem empowered by blockchain, pioneering an intelligent future.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

SAN FRANCISCO, Wednesday, May 13 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, PG&E Corporation closed lower the previous trading day. PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG), the NYSE's 6th largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, dropped 1.2% during its previous trading session to close at $16.60.

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) operates as a leading California-based utility provider offering electrical and natural gas services. PG&E's integral role in California's energy infrastructure is underpinned by its strategic initiatives in grid modernization and the expansion of renewable energy sources, including nuclear energy through the Diablo Canyon nuclear plant. The company is proactive in addressing wildfire liabilities with comprehensive risk management strategies and policy compliance. As industry dynamics shift towards cleaner energy, PG&E's focus on regulatory policies and safety standards strengthens its competitive positioning, enhancing long-term value for PG&E stock investors and aligning with sustainability goals.

Wed Apr 15, 2026: PG&E Secures NRC License Renewal for Diablo Canyon Power Plant

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) has received a significant boost as it secured approval from the federal Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) for a 20-year license renewal for its Diablo Canyon Power Plant. This approval, announced on April 2, 2026, comes after a thorough review process that confirmed the facility's safety and environmental soundness for continued operations until 2046. However, extending operations beyond 2030 will require further action from the California Legislature.

The NRC's endorsement marks a critical milestone for PG&E, especially as the company navigates complex regulatory landscapes and market dynamics. On 15th April 2026, Jefferies analyst Julien Dumoulin-Smith downgraded PG&E Corporation from Buy to Hold, adjusting the price target to $19 from $20. This decision stems from diminishing confidence in wildfire liability reforms in California, with Dumoulin-Smith noting that alignment among utilities and insurers remains tenuous, casting uncertainty over potential political support for structural changes.

In contrast, JPMorgan has taken a more optimistic stance on PG&E Corporation, raising its price target on the shares to $24 from a previous $21 while maintaining an Overweight rating. The bank's revised outlook reflects updates to its models for the North America utilities sector, suggesting a more favorable view of PG&E's financial trajectory amidst ongoing challenges.

PG&E Corporation, which provides electricity and natural gas services across California, faces a dual challenge of operational regulatory compliance and market perceptions. While the recent NRC approval is a positive development, the company must also address investor concerns about wildfire risk and related liabilities. This complex environment necessitates a careful navigation of both operational and legislative hurdles to ensure long-term viability.

As PG&E Corporation moves forward, investors will be closely watching not just the implications of the Diablo Canyon license renewal, but also the broader context of California's regulatory framework and the evolving landscape of utility operations. While there is notable potential for growth and stability within PG&E, some analysts point to alternative investment opportunities, particularly in the AI sector, which may present greater upside and reduced risk.

- As per the Du Pont analysis, Return on Equity of 8.2% is lower than sector average of 9.9%. This is computed as net profit margin of 10.8% times asset turnover [sales/assets] of 0.18 times leverage factor [total assets/shareholders' equity] of 4.3.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

PG&E Corporation, a holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates as a major energy utility provider in California through its subsidiary, Pacific Gas and Electric Company. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity from diverse sources, including natural gas, hydroelectric, nuclear, and increasingly renewables, alongside the transportation and delivery of natural gas to residential, commercial, and industrial customers across Northern and Central California. The company's fastest-growing segments include renewable energy integration, such as solar and wind projects, driven by California's aggressive clean energy targets, as well as grid modernization initiatives like advanced metering infrastructure and support for electric vehicle charging networks. These areas are fueled by rising demand for sustainable solutions, regulatory incentives, and technological advancements, positioning PG&E for long-term growth in a transitioning energy landscape. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) operates as a Pacific Gas & Electric stock, recognized for its PG&E regulated utility services, providing PG&E electric service California and PG&E natural gas service. As a major player in the region, the PG&E stock is often scrutinized for its PG&E earnings, PG&E dividend, and PG&E stock forecast. With a PG&E market cap reflecting its significant presence, the company's ongoing PG&E safety program and PG&E ESG initiatives aim to enhance its reputation and tackle PG&E wildfire mitigation alongside PG&E wildfire liabilities. Following PG&E bankruptcy exit and PG&E legal settlements, the PCG investment thesis has been cautiously optimistic. Recent PCG analyst update and PG&E analyst rating influence the PG&E price target, with the BMO price target PG&E being a key indicator. Investors also focus on PG&E price target BMO Capital as part of PG&E investment strategies. Continuous PG&E infrastructure upgrades, including PG&E grid modernization and PG&E smart meter implementation, bolster its operational resilience, aided by systematic PG&E power outage updates. Consequently, the latest PG&E shareholder update features strategic insights on PG&E rate case adjustments.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

SAN DIEGO, Wednesday, May 13 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Sempra closed lower the previous trading day. Sempra (NYSE:SRE), the NYSE's 4th largest Utility company by market cap, dropped 1.8% during its previous trading session to close at $91.68.

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) is an American energy infrastructure company specializing in the development and operation of energy networks. The company is engaged in the construction and management of electricity grids and natural gas pipelines, offering reliable energy delivery services through its extensive network. Sempra's strategic investments in renewable energy projects and innovative technology solutions highlight its commitment to sustainability. With a robust pipeline of projects focused on clean energy transition, Sempra leverages its large-scale ecosystem and technological leadership. Key competitive advantages include its expansive supply chain and ability to innovate in the energy sector.

SECTION 1 RECENT NEWS

Mon May 11, 2026: Sempra Moves Closer to Initial LNG Production at ECA Terminal

Sempra is on the brink of initiating LNG production at its Energ a Costa Azul (ECA) facility in Mexico this June, marking a significant stride in its energy infrastructure expansion. As of the 11th of May 2026, the facility has begun receiving feed gas as of April 20, positioning itself for full operations at a capacity of 3.25 million tons per annum, according to Industrial Info Resources. This development enhances Sempra's competitive edge in the North American LNG export landscape, with gas sourced primarily from the US Permian Basin.

The company's first-quarter 2026 earnings will be disclosed on May 7, providing investors with a clearer picture of Sempra's financial performance in light of these operational advancements. The ongoing progress at the ECA terminal is a crucial element of Sempra's strategy to bolster its LNG export capabilities, which are vital as global demand for LNG continues to surge due to energy transition initiatives worldwide.

Sempra has established itself as a leading player in North American energy infrastructure, focusing on both utilities and LNG development. Its diversified portfolio includes regulated utility operations in California and Texas, complemented by the growing importance of LNG projects like ECA. The company's emphasis on infrastructure investment aims to meet the increasing energy demands while ensuring safety and operational excellence, as outlined in its investor communications.

The LNG sector is witnessing heightened demand driven by global energy transitions, positioning Sempra favorably as it capitalizes on its US-Mexico gateway. The strategic location of the ECA terminal on the Pacific Coast offers logistical advantages for exports to Asian markets, distinguishing it from competitors based on the Gulf Coast. Furthermore, Sempra's significant operations in Texas link it closely to the overall economic growth of the United States, solidifying its role in the energy sector.

For US investors, Sempra offers a unique opportunity to gain exposure to stable utility operations and a high-growth LNG segment without direct international risk. With its sourcing from the Permian Basin and strong presence in Texas, Sempra is well-positioned to navigate through domestic market volatility while ensuring sustained growth. As the company approaches the commencement of production at the ECA terminal and releases its quarterly earnings, investors are keenly observing these developments for insights into Sempra's long-term strategic positioning.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 341 words

Sun May 10, 2026: Sempra Shares Climb Following $10 Billion Infrastructure Stake Sale

Sempra's stock price surged nearly 5% on the New York Stock Exchange following the announcement of a significant divestiture. On 10th May 2026, the San Diego-based utility holding company revealed it would sell a 45% interest in its infrastructure business to private equity firm KKR for $10 billion. This strategic move is aimed at simplifying Sempra's business model and enhancing its financial position.

The infrastructure unit being sold includes operations related to liquefied natural gas terminals, gas pipelines, and renewable energy facilities such as solar and wind generation sites. Jeffrey W. Martin, Sempra's chairman and CEO, emphasized that the transaction is designed to streamline operations and fund robust utility growth in key markets like Texas and California. He stated that this sale underscores Sempra's commitment to a long-term partnership with KKR, aligning their vision to strengthen America's global leadership in LNG exports.

Upon completion of the deal, a KKR-led consortium will hold a 65% equity stake in Sempra Infrastructure Partners, while Sempra will maintain a 25% interest and the Abu Dhabi Investment Authority will acquire the remaining 10%. Sempra indicated that the transaction will result in approximately 95% of its earnings coming from regulated U.S. utilities, significantly reducing the need for future equity issuances to support capital improvements.

This sale marks a pivotal shift in Sempra's strategic focus, allowing the company to concentrate on its core utility operations while simultaneously bolstering its financial strength. The transaction is expected to enhance Sempra's ability to invest in its regulated utility businesses, which are crucial for growth in the evolving energy landscape. Analysts are closely watching how this move will impact Sempra's long-term growth trajectory and market positioning.

Investors are encouraged by the news, as the stock's upward movement reflects confidence in Sempra's strategic direction. The collaboration with KKR, a well-established player in the private equity space, could open new avenues for future investments and operational efficiencies. As Sempra continues to navigate the complexities of the energy market, this transaction may serve as a catalyst for further growth and innovation in the utility sector.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 239 words

Sat May 9, 2026: Sempra Targets 11% Utility Rate Base Growth by 2030

Sempra, a leading energy infrastructure company, is embarking on a strategic simplification of its business model to prioritize utility operations. This shift is expected to drive an 11% annual rate base growth through the year 2030, as announced on 11th May 2026.

A key factor contributing to Sempra's recent performance is the Oncor base rate review settlement. This settlement establishes a higher authorized equity layer of 43.5% and a return on equity (ROE) of 9.75%, which aligns rates more closely with the current cost environment, thereby enhancing profitability.

Texas has emerged as a focal point for infrastructure development, driven by surging demand from artificial intelligence and data centers. In response, Sempra has initiated a record $65 billion capital plan, with a significant portion of the rate base expected to be concentrated in Texas by 2030, positioning the company favorably for future growth.

To support this ambitious capital deployment, operational modernization at Oncor is underway, which includes diversifying supply chains. Sempra has secured labor and materials through 2028 to mitigate execution risks, ensuring that the company remains on track with its development goals.

In addition, Sempra Infrastructure is poised for significant near-term revenue contributions. The first production of liquefied natural gas (LNG) at ECA Phase 1 is scheduled for next month, with substantial completion anticipated by this summer. Furthermore, management has identified approximately $9 billion in incremental capital opportunities, primarily in Texas, driven by the rapid growth of large-load customers.

SECTION 5 CORPORATE PROFILE

5.1 Activities

Sempra, a leading energy infrastructure company listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: SRE), is a Fortune 500 holding entity headquartered in San Diego, California. The company's core business revolves around the development, operation, and investment in energy infrastructure, including natural gas utilities, pipelines, storage facilities, and electric services across North America. Through subsidiaries like San Diego Gas & Electric and Southern California Gas Company, Sempra delivers reliable energy to millions of customers, emphasizing safety, sustainability, and operational efficiency. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects and liquefied natural gas (LNG) exports, driven by increasing demand for clean energy solutions and global market expansion. For instance, Sempra's investments in wind, solar, and battery storage, as well as its LNG facilities in Mexico and the U.S., have fueled significant growth, positioning the company as a key player in the energy transition. With a strong focus on innovation and strategic partnerships, Sempra continues to enhance its It is the NYSE's 4th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) is strategically positioned as a powerhouse in the energy sector, leveraging a diversified business model focused on regulated utility rate increase dynamics and robust investments in energy infrastructure. As a key player, Sempra's comprehensive base rate approval, specifically the Oncor base rate review approval, strengthens its influence in the Texas electric utility base rate landscape. The company's operations, encompassing electric transmission and distribution services and natural gas pipelines and storage, showcase its commitment to energy infrastructure growth. Significant grid modernization at Oncor, paired with reliable utility capital expenditures (capex), underscores Sempra's proactive approach in enhancing transmission and distribution investment. Additionally, renewable energy projects Sempra, along with battery storage projects Sempra, highlight its dedication to sustainable energy solutions. With competitive strengths in Sempra regulated utilities (SoCalGas SDG&E Oncor) and a focus on regulated rate base expansion, Sempra offers promising avenues for Sempra investor earnings growth amid regulatory approvals and utility revenue requirement approval.

SAN FRANCISCO, Thursday, May 14 2026 - PG&E Corporation's (NYSE: PCG $16.81) stock price lifted 21.0c (1.3%) from its previous trading session to close at $16.81.

Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 61.5 points (0.8%) in the day, the relative price increase was 0.4%.

There were 12,158,426 shares worth $204.4 million traded today; the volume was 0.5 times the average daily trading volume of 23.2 million shares.

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) operates as a leading California-based utility provider offering electrical and natural gas services. PG&E's integral role in California's energy infrastructure is underpinned by its strategic initiatives in grid modernization and the expansion of renewable energy sources, including nuclear energy through the Diablo Canyon nuclear plant. The company is proactive in addressing wildfire liabilities with comprehensive risk management strategies and policy compliance. As industry dynamics shift towards cleaner energy, PG&E's focus on regulatory policies and safety standards strengthens its competitive positioning, enhancing long-term value for PG&E stock investors and aligning with sustainability goals.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

PG&E Corporation, a holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates as a major energy utility provider in California through its subsidiary, Pacific Gas and Electric Company. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity from diverse sources, including natural gas, hydroelectric, nuclear, and increasingly renewables, alongside the transportation and delivery of natural gas to residential, commercial, and industrial customers across Northern and Central California. The company's fastest-growing segments include renewable energy integration, such as solar and wind projects, driven by California's aggressive clean energy targets, as well as grid modernization initiatives like advanced metering infrastructure and support for electric vehicle charging networks. These areas are fueled by rising demand for sustainable solutions, regulatory incentives, and technological advancements, positioning PG&E for long-term growth in a transitioning energy landscape. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) operates as a Pacific Gas & Electric stock, recognized for its PG&E regulated utility services, providing PG&E electric service California and PG&E natural gas service. As a major player in the region, the PG&E stock is often scrutinized for its PG&E earnings, PG&E dividend, and PG&E stock forecast. With a PG&E market cap reflecting its significant presence, the company's ongoing PG&E safety program and PG&E ESG initiatives aim to enhance its reputation and tackle PG&E wildfire mitigation alongside PG&E wildfire liabilities. Following PG&E bankruptcy exit and PG&E legal settlements, the PCG investment thesis has been cautiously optimistic. Recent PCG analyst update and PG&E analyst rating influence the PG&E price target, with the BMO price target PG&E being a key indicator. Investors also focus on PG&E price target BMO Capital as part of PG&E investment strategies. Continuous PG&E infrastructure upgrades, including PG&E grid modernization and PG&E smart meter implementation, bolster its operational resilience, aided by systematic PG&E power outage updates. Consequently, the latest PG&E shareholder update features strategic insights on PG&E rate case adjustments.

SECTION 3 PAST YEAR PRESS RELEASES, TOP 10 STORIES & PAST MONTH ECONOMIC NEWS

3.1 Press Releases and Corporate Wire

Press Release article 1 of 5, Source: WEC, 75 words

February 24: PG&E quarterly dividends steady

PG&E today announced a quarterly dividend of 5.0c per share for Q4/2025; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q3/2025) dividend of 5.0c. The ex-dividend date is Tuesday, March 31, 2026 and the record date is Tuesday, March 31, 2026 and it is payable on Wednesday, April 15. Total dividends per share paid in the 12 months ended February 24 were 12.50c.

Press Release article 2 of 5, Source: Company Website, 213 words

January 15: Events Calendar: PG&E Corporation Schedules Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Earnings Release and Conference Call

/PRNewswire/ PG&E Corporation (NYSE: PCG) will hold a conference call on Thursday, February 12, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. Eastern Time (8:00 a.m. Pacific Time) to discuss its fourth quarter and full year 2025 results. The public can access the conference call through a simultaneous webcast. The link is provided below and will also be available from the PG&E Corporation website.

PG&E Corporation Logo (PRNewsfoto/PG&E Corporation)

What: Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Earnings Call

When: Thursday, February 12, 2026 at 11:00 a.m. Eastern Time

Where: http://investor.pgecorp.com/news-events/events-and-presentations/default.aspx

A replay of the live conference call and webcast will be available at http://investor.pgecorp.com/news-events/events-and-presentations/default.aspx.

Please contact Investor Relations at invrel@pge-corp.com if you have any questions.

PG &E Corporation (NYSE: PCG) is a holding company headquartered in Oakland. It is the parent company of Pacific Gas and Electric Company, an energy company that serves 16 million Californians across a 70,000-square-mile service area in Northern and Central California. For more information, visit http://www.pgecorp.com.

Source: Company Website

Press Release article 3 of 5, Source: Company Website, 301 words

January 12: More Than $300,000 in College Scholarships Funded by PG&E Coworkers Now Available for Students in Northern and Central California

/PRNewswire/ Pacific Gas and Electric Company (PG&E) announced today that scholarship applications are now being accepted for college-bound high schoolers as well as current college and continuing education students with a primary residence in PG&E's service area in Northern and Central California.

Pacific Gas and Electric Company (PRNewsfoto/Pacific Gas and Electric Company)

More than $300,000 is available through scholarships created and funded by PG&E's 11 employee resource groups (ERGs) and two engineering network groups (ENGs). These groups help advance a culture at PG&E where all coworkers are valued, included and supported. The funds are raised entirely through coworker donations, coworker fundraising events and the company's coworker giving program.

These scholarships are awarded annually to help offset the cost of higher education. ERG and ENG scholarship winners will each receive awards ranging from $1,000 to $6,000 for exemplary scholastic achievement and community leadership.

PG&E ERG and ENG scholarship information, including criteria and applications, is available on PG&E's website. To be considered for a scholarship, all applications must be submitted by Feb. 28.

"Our ERG and ENG scholarships support the ambitions and dreams of young people in the hometowns we serve. They help open doors to develop future leaders and breakthrough thinkers. The scholarships are part of how we're living our purpose at PG&E-delivering for our hometowns, serving our planet, and leading with love," said Deb Sinta, PG&E Senior Vice President, Talent, Culture and People.

Voices from past recipients

"I hope to work as either a software engineer or cybersecurity analyst to ensure a world where everyone may stay secure in this digital age," said 2025 ERG scholarship recipient Lina Kaci, a computer science major at UC Berkeley.

Press Release article 4 of 5, 359 words

December 31 2025: PG&E: File SEC Form 10-K - Management's Discussion and Analysis 10-K

MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

OVERVIEW

This is a combined report of PG&E Corporation and the Utility and includes separate Consolidated Financial Statements for each of these two entities. This combined MD&A should be read in conjunction with the Consolidated Financial Statements and the Notes to the Consolidated Financial Statements included in Item 8.

Generally, PG&E Corporation's and the Utility's revenues vary based on the outcomes of ratemaking proceedings and the amount of pass-through costs incurred. See "Ratemaking Mechanisms" in Item 1. Description of the Business regarding how the Utility's revenues are determined. Factors that cause costs to vary include the cost of purchased power and fuel; the costs of procurement storage, transportation of natural gas; weather; criminal, civil and regulatory charges for wildfires; the outcomes of ratemaking proceedings; and increases in interest expense as a result of additional debt issuances.

The discussion related to the results of operations and liquidity for 2024 compared to 2023 is incorporated by reference to Part II, Item 7. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations in PG&E Corporation's and the Utility's combined Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2024, which was filed with the SEC in February 2025.

Key Factors Affecting Financial Results

PG&E Corporation and the Utility believe that their financial condition, results of operations, liquidity, and cash flows may be materially affected by the following factors:

The Uncertainties in Connection with Wildfires, Wildfire Mitigation, and Associated Cost Recovery. PG&E Corporation's and the Utility's financial condition, results of operations, liquidity, and cash flows may be materially affected by the costs and effectiveness of the Utility's wildfire mitigation initiatives; the extent of damages from wildfires that do occur; the financial impacts of wildfires; and PG&E Corporation's and the Utility's ability to mitigate those financial impacts with insurance, self-insurance, the Wildfire Fund, the Continuation Account, and regulatory recovery.

In response to the wildfire threat facing California, PG&E Corporation and the Utility have taken aggressive steps designed to mitigate the threat of catastrophic wildfires.

Press Release article 5 of 5, Source: Company Website, 320 words

December 31 2025: PG&E Donates $1 Million to Local Food Banks to Feed Families

Contribution is Third this Year as Need Rises

OAKLAND, Calif., Dec. 31, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Just in time for the holidays, Pacific Gas and Electric Company (PG&E) is contributing $1 million to local food banks to help feed the growing number of individuals and families struggling to put food on the table.

Pacific Gas and Electric Company (PRNewsfoto/Pacific Gas and Electric Company)

The PG&E funding will provide the equivalent of approximately 3 million meals for individuals and families. It will support 38 food banks representing 47 counties in PG&E's Northern and Central California service area.

It is the third contribution to food banks since September from PG&E or The PG&E Corporation Foundation (the PG&E Foundation), bringing the total for community food support this year to $2.37 million. Funding for these charitable contributions comes from PG&E shareholders, not PG&E customers.

Officials with the California Association of Food Banks said that food banks are facing record-breaking demand, the highest since the pandemic.

"California food banks experienced an unexpected surge with the [federal government] shutdown this fall. So, we reached out for help on their behalf and PG&E responded," said Stacia Levenfeld, Chief Executive Officer of California Association of Food Banks. "Their $1 million gift to food banks throughout Northern and Central California will have a meaningful impact on the lives of millions of people this holiday season and help food banks continue their critical work in our communities."

"We are grateful to help local food banks fulfill their mission during this time of increasing demand, especially as more families and seniors are struggling through the holiday season. Our longstanding partnership with the California Association of Food Banks supports the safety net that is our local food banks," said Carla Peterman, Executive Vice President, Corporate Affairs, PG&E Corporation and Chair of The PG&E Corporation Foundation Board.

To view more press releases from the past year, please access the link provided below.

http://www.buysellsignals.com/bst/015991961011405261160

3.2 PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

Story 1 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 104 words

Record $73 Billion Capital Investment Plan Through 2030 Announced

PG&E has unveiled a historic $73 billion five-year capital investment plan extending through 2030, marking a $10 billion increase over previous guidance. The strategy targets 9% annual rate base growth to support grid modernization, wildfire mitigation, and infrastructure for growing data center demand. Despite significant investment, CEO Patti Poppe projects flat or declining customer bills in 2027, achieved through sustained operational efficiencies including a 2% annual reduction in operations and maintenance costs. The plan allocates $20 billion to FERC-regulated transmission upgrades and funds distribution improvements without requiring new equity issuance, aiming to maintain investment-grade credit ratings and support at least 9% annual EPS growth.

Story 2 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 95 words

Fitch Ratings Upgrades PG&E to Investment Grade ('BBB-') with Stable Outlook

In September 2025, Fitch Ratings upgraded PG&E to investment-grade status ('BBB-') with a stable outlook, citing material improvements in wildfire risk mitigation and the regulatory protections afforded by Senate Bill 254. The rating agency highlighted the legislation's shift of the disallowance cap determination to the date of ignition as a key factor in reducing financial liability exposure. This upgrade signals strengthened credit fundamentals, enabling the utility to service debt at lower rates and benefit customer affordability. Fitch expects continued credit metric improvements, provided the company sustains its mitigation performance and the regulatory environment remains stable.

Story 3 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 86 words

Senate Bill 254 Wildfire Fund Expansion Creates $18 Billion Continuation Account and Enhanced Protections

California's enactment of Senate Bill 254 in September 2025 established an $18 billion wildfire fund continuation account, significantly refining the state's liability framework. The legislation reduces PG&E's financial exposure by relocating the disallowance cap determination to the ignition date and eliminating upfront utility contributions in favor of contingent obligations. Additionally, it lowers PG&E's annual fund contribution by approximately 25% starting in 2029. The bill also authorizes a second phase study to explore broader climate-driven risk allocation policies, demonstrating ongoing legislative commitment to utility sector financial stability.

Story 4 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 77 words

Q3 2025 Earnings Beat EPS Expectations Amid Operational Efficiency Gains

PG&E reported third-quarter 2025 core earnings per share of $0.50, exceeding analyst consensus by 16.3% despite a 2.5% revenue miss. The performance was driven largely by effective cost management, including sustained reductions in inspection and vendor costs, alongside favorable tax planning. Year-to-date earnings of $1.14 position the company to meet its full-year guidance, which forecasts 10% growth over 2024. Management introduced 2026 EPS guidance indicating continued 9% growth, supported by the extended capital plan and capital-efficient operations.

Story 5 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 83 words

Department of Energy Closes $15 Billion Loan Guarantee for Project Polaris Grid Modernization

The Department of Energy finalized a $15 billion conditional loan guarantee for PG&E in January 2025 to finance "Project Polaris," a comprehensive grid modernization initiative. The low-cost financing covers hydropower expansion, battery storage, transmission upgrades, and virtual power plant development, with estimated customer savings of over $1 billion during the repayment period. The deal includes community benefit provisions focusing on workforce development and equity. Management views the loan as critical for addressing capacity expansion needs driven by artificial intelligence and data center growth.

Story 6 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 92 words

Diablo Canyon Nuclear Power Plant Officially Enters Full Extended Operations Through 2030

Diablo Canyon's Unit 2 began extended operations in August 2025, joining Unit 1 to ensure the facility remains online through at least 2030. This extension addresses California's grid reliability needs and surging electricity demand from data centers, contributing approximately 20% of the state's clean energy. The plant serves as a vital economic engine for San LuisObispo County and maintains top-tier safety ratings from the NRC. While license renewal for operations through 2045 is pending state agency approvals, continued operation is projected to save ratepayers billions by avoiding costly alternative infrastructure investments.

Story 7 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 87 words

Calistoga Resiliency Center Hybrid Microgrid Achieves Commercial Operation with 293 MWh Capacity

PG&E and Energy Vault commenced commercial operations of the Calistoga Resiliency Center in September 2025. This 293 MWh hybrid microgrid combines hydrogen fuel cells and lithium-ion batteries to provide 8.5 MW of capacity, capable of powering approximately 1,600 customers for at least 48 hours during outages. Designed to function independently from the main grid, the facility protects critical community infrastructure in a high-fire-threat area. The project represents a scalable model for zero-emissions resilience technology and is part of a broader deployment of community microgrids across PG&E's territory.

Story 8 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 83 words

Data Center Power Demand Pipeline Grows to 10 Gigawatts Over Next Decade

PG&E's pipeline for data center power demand has nearly tripled year-over-year to 10 gigawatts, with 1.6 gigawatts in final engineering stages scheduled for operation between 2026 and 2030. The demand is concentrated in smaller, inference-model projects clustered in Northern California, capitalizing on the region's fiber infrastructure. Management estimates that every gigawatt of new load could reduce customer bills by 1-2% through rate base growth. While current capital planning remains conservative, the robust pipeline offers significant potential upside for future investment and infrastructure expansion.

Story 9 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 90 words

Strategic Partnership with Smart Wires Deploys Advanced Power Flow Control Technology at Los Esteros Substation

PG&E has partnered with Smart Wires to deploy advanced power flow control technology at the Los Esteros substation by late 2025. The installation of SmartValve devices will unlock over 100 MW of additional capacity in the San Jose area by mitigating thermal overloads on existing transmission lines. This solution was selected for its speed and cost-effectiveness compared to traditional infrastructure expansion, serving as a bridge until a permanent transmission line is completed in 2032. The project underscores a strategic focus on deploying innovative grid-enhancing technologies to manage rapid load growth.

Story 10 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 82 words

CEO Patti Poppe Secures Five-Year Contract Extension Through 2030, Ensuring Leadership Continuity

PG&E's Board extended CEO Patti Poppe's contract through 2030 to ensure leadership stability during a pivotal phase of operational recovery and capital execution. The extension aims to mitigate risks associated with executive transitions and preserve institutional knowledge essential for regulatory engagement and strategic initiatives. Poppe's tenure has been characterized by consistent cost reduction achievements, improved brand trust, and effective capital planning. The move signals Board confidence in her direction and provides long-term visibility for stakeholders regarding the company's financial and operational priorities.

3.3 ECONOMIC NEWS

ECO NEWS 1 of 1, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 212 words

Apr 22, 2026: Anterix-Benton PUD Spectrum Agreement Strengthens Utility Private-LTE Ecosystem Supporting PG&E's Wildfire Mitigation and Grid Hardening

On April 21, 2026, Anterix Inc. disclosed that its subsidiary PDV Spectrum Holding Company, LLC, entered into a $771,000 spectrum license sale agreement with Public Utility District No. 1 of Benton County in Washington for a 10 MHz 900 MHz broadband license. Industry reports indicate the utility-owned network will serve a 927-square-mile territory covering more than 58,000 customers.

Market data indicates the agreement represents the first private wireless broadband deployment among publicly owned utilities in the Pacific Northwest. The deployment supports grid automation, outage detection and restoration, and field workforce communications aligned with clean-energy compliance requirements.

Impact on PG&E: PCG

Following this development, PG&E Corporation, through Pacific Gas and Electric Company, gains another operational reference relevant to its private wireless investments across Northern and Central California. Operational metrics indicate PG&E serves approximately 16 million people across 70,000 square miles and holds an existing Anterix 900 MHz spectrum agreement, deploying private LTE infrastructure across high fire-threat districts.

Analysis shows the Benton PUD agreement strengthens the ecosystem supporting PG&E's deployment, including device availability, tower partner networks, and cybersecurity frameworks. Expanded adoption also reinforces regulatory familiarity before the California Public Utilities Commission reviewing PG&E's wildfire mitigation plans and undergrounding commitments.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

SAN DIEGO, Wednesday, May 13 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Sempra closed lower the previous trading day. Sempra (NYSE:SRE), the NYSE's 4th largest Utility company by market cap, dropped 1.8% during its previous trading session to close at $91.68.

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) is an American energy infrastructure company specializing in the development and operation of energy networks. The company is engaged in the construction and management of electricity grids and natural gas pipelines, offering reliable energy delivery services through its extensive network. Sempra's strategic investments in renewable energy projects and innovative technology solutions highlight its commitment to sustainability. With a robust pipeline of projects focused on clean energy transition, Sempra leverages its large-scale ecosystem and technological leadership. Key competitive advantages include its expansive supply chain and ability to innovate in the energy sector.
