+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP APPOINTS CARLY REINER CACERES AS NATIONAL CAMPUS RECRUITING HR DIRECTOR

Clark Construction Group appointed Carly Reiner Caceres as Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting, effective Apr. 29, 2026, with a mandate to advance national campus recruiting initiatives and strengthen early-career talent pipelines for its broad portfolio of projects.Caceres arrives from Proper, where she served as Vice President of People Operations, shaping HR strategy and operations in a hybrid workplace. Her background also includes leadership roles at MILLER as Director of Internal Operations and more than nine years at Accenture as Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Her prior experience at Grant Thornton LLP overseeing campus recruiting strategies further reinforces her capability to build robust internship programs and solid student engagement initiatives.Clark Construction Group, established in 1906, has a long track record across education and infrastructure markets. The company says Caceres will bring strategic HR expertise to attract top graduates and mentor the next generation of construction professionals.Her early career featured internships at Charleston Magazine and the Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum, rounding out a diverse background in communications and external relations that will help guide talent development efforts.

+ RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL AWARDS MULTI-TRADES FRAMEWORK CONTRACT TO CLARK CONTRACTS FOR DOMESTIC HOUSING WORKS

Clark Contracts has been appointed to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades contractors framework to improve delivery of repair, maintenance and refurbishment services for domestic properties across the authority. The appointment was announced on 11th February 2026 and the framework runs through 2027.The framework covers a broad range of multi-trade works, enabling the council to manage both responsive and planned maintenance more effectively across its housing stock and ensuring residents benefit from a faster, more reliable service.Clark Contracts is a well-established public-sector supplier, with current agreements including Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework. The company also participates in the Crown Commercial Service’s Construction Framework and several Scottish Procurement Alliance frameworks.Gordon Cunningham, managing director at Clark Contracts, said the appointment builds on a track record of more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over the past three years and demonstrates the firm’s commitment to serving local communities.The deal supports Clark Contracts’ growing public-sector footprint as it works with councils and institutions such as the University of Glasgow and Glasgow City Council to deliver essential housing services.

+ CDC APPROVES AMENDMENT FOR ₱4-B THE VILLAGES DEVELOPMENT, PKR JOINS CLARK GROWTH DRIVE

Clark Freeport is set to host a ₱4-billion mixed-use residential and commercial development in The Villages after Clark Development Corp. approved an amendment to PKR Corp.'s lease on December 13, 2025. The revised lease provides PKR with a 30-month window to complete construction and includes safeguards aimed at ensuring on-time delivery. Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera, CDC president and CEO, noted that the amendment reinforces investor confidence and the efficient execution of projects, while keeping The Villages as a community hub where residents can live and participate. PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung thanked CDC for the partnership and said the company will deliver the Clark project, with PKR operating under Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean firm known for safety, technology, and workplace practices. The initiative fits CDC's broader push to accelerate investments, promote well-planned communities, and establish a business-friendly environment that appeals to both global and local investors.

value.Title: EDP caught in the crossfire between California and Trump

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ0-XM13-S4GT-B15X-00000-00EDP caught in the crossfire between California and Trump2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:41:31ZLexisNexisEDP caught in the crossfire between California and Trump


EDP has found itself caught in the middle of the war between the state of California and Donald Trump. At its core, this is a battle between two visions for America's future. California would be the world's fourth-largest economy if it were a country. The "Golden State" believes in a future powered by green energy, led by Democratic Governor Gavin Newsom, one of Trump's leading critics and a potential presidential candidate in 2028. The U.S. president, on the other hand, is betting everything on the continuation of fossil fuels, and his offensive against wind energy remains unstoppable, having already described wind turbines as "ugly" or "monsters."


The investigation was launched this week by the California Energy Commission (CEC), a state agency, regarding the agreement signed between the U.S. government and the consortium that led to the cancellation of the Golden State Wind offshore wind project, with 2 gigawatts of capacity off the central California coast. This project was owned by the Ocean Winds consortium (EDPR + Engie) along with the British company Reventus Power and the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board (CPP Investments).
"The Trump administration is irresponsibly spending billions of taxpayer dollars on backroom deals that set innovation back," said David Hochschild, president of the CEC. "Californians deserve immediate answers about the nature of this payment. Taxpayer money should be used to build a sustainable energy future, not to pay to make projects disappear."
The state agency has thus launched an investigation to determine whether there were "potential violations of the law" in the Trump administration's agreement.
When contacted by JE, EDP declined to comment.
The agreement provides for the return of $120 million to the consortium, but this amount must be reinvested in fossil fuel projects on the Gulf Coast of Mexico, in the southern part of the country, with EDP guaranteeing that it will not participate in these initiatives, leaving them in the hands of partners.
The company assures that the U.S. will remain one of its key markets, but now with a focus on solar energy and batteries.
Donald Trump has been on a rampage of canceling wind projects since returning to the White House. In late 2025, the federal government announced the suspension of all permits for large offshore wind projects under construction, citing risks to national security but without detailing them.
To avoid litigation with companies in court, it opted for a new strategy. It is offering to convert the value of the licenses into funds to invest in fossil fuel projects. The first agreement of this kind was signed in March with France's Total Energies, which will invest 860 million euros in a liquefied natural gas plant in Texas.
In addition to the project in California, another consortium in which EDPR is involved also reached an agreement to cancel the Bluepoint Wind project on the country's east coast, with one of the partners set to invest over 700 million in a gas project in Texas. The White House has described the projects as "expensive, unreliable, and intermittent." Of the three offshore wind projects in the U.S., EDP now has only one: SouthCoast Wind off the coast of Massachusetts, which is currently on hold.

urn:contentItem:6JHY-RN03-RXHG-54BF-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: MP Materials Corp. reports Revenue of $133m2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:41:31ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: MP Materials Corp. reports Revenue of $133m

AMERICAN RESULTS

MP Materials Corp. (NYSE:MP) announced total revenue for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026] of $133m.

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Total revenue was up 118.6% from $60.8m to $132.9m

Unfavourable Changes:

- Loss of $8.0m

Note:

- Tax expenses to Sales was down from 7.4% to 4.3%

Quarterly Report

March 3120262025ChangeDescription$ m$ m(%)Total Revenue132.960.8Up 118.6EBIT(13.6)(27.2)ImprovementPre Tax Profit/(Loss)(13.6)(27.2)ImprovementProfit/(Loss) after Tax(8)(22.6)ImprovementNet Assets1,967.11,036.1Up 89.9

Per Share figuresMarch 3120262025Change (%)Description$$EPS(4c)(14c)Improvement

ACTIVITIES:

MP Materials Corp., a U.S.-based company listed on the NYSE, is a leading producer of rare earth elements, essential for advanced technologies. Its core business involves mining, processing, and refining rare earth materials at its Mountain Pass facility in California, the only active rare earth mine in the United States. The company focuses on producing high-purity rare earth oxides and neodymium-iron-boron magnets, which are critical components in electric vehicles, wind turbines, and consumer electronics. The fastest-growing segments include the supply of materials for the electric vehicle market and renewable energy applications, driven by increasing global demand for sustainable technologies. MP Materials is strategically positioned to capitalize on supply chain diversification efforts, emphasizing domestic production to reduce reliance on foreign sources, thereby supporting the broader transition to a low-carbon economy. Through vertical integration and innovation, the company aims to enhance efficiency and expand its market presence in high-growth sectors.

MARKET POSITION:

MP Materials Corp. Common Stock (NYSE:MP), the NYSE's 2nd largest Metals and Mining company by market cap, jumped $18.64 (or 36.9%) year-to-date (YTD) in 2026 to close at $69.13. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 7.2% YTD, this is a relative price increase of 29.7%. A zero-bagger in 2025, the value of USD1,000 invested two years ago is $4,294 [vs $1,417 for the S&P 500 Index], for a capital gain of $3,294. The average annualised return to shareholders has been 107.2% in the past two years. The average daily volume of 4.9 million shares so far in 2026 was 0.5 times the average daily volume in 2025.

SAN DIEGO, Friday, May 08 2026 - Sempra's (NYSE: SRE $91.53) stock price slipped further on Friday, sliding an additional 4.0c (0.04%) to close at $91.53.

This brought its 6-day fall to 3.8%; compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 187.6 points (2.6%) in the six days for a relative price change of -6.4%.

Cumulative volume in the 6 days was 0.8 times the average 6-day volume of 3.7 million shares. There were 3,369,600 shares worth $308.4 million traded today.

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) is an American energy infrastructure company specializing in the development and operation of energy networks. The company is engaged in the construction and management of electricity grids and natural gas pipelines, offering reliable energy delivery services through its extensive network. Sempra's strategic investments in renewable energy projects and innovative technology solutions highlight its commitment to sustainability. With a robust pipeline of projects focused on clean energy transition, Sempra leverages its large-scale ecosystem and technological leadership. Key competitive advantages include its expansive supply chain and ability to innovate in the energy sector.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Sempra, a leading energy infrastructure company listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: SRE), is a Fortune 500 holding entity headquartered in San Diego, California. The company's core business revolves around the development, operation, and investment in energy infrastructure, including natural gas utilities, pipelines, storage facilities, and electric services across North America. Through subsidiaries like San Diego Gas & Electric and Southern California Gas Company, Sempra delivers reliable energy to millions of customers, emphasizing safety, sustainability, and operational efficiency. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects and liquefied natural gas (LNG) exports, driven by increasing demand for clean energy solutions and global market expansion. For instance, Sempra's investments in wind, solar, and battery storage, as well as its LNG facilities in Mexico and the U.S., have fueled significant growth, positioning the company as a key player in the energy transition. With a strong focus on innovation and strategic partnerships, Sempra continues to enhance its It is the NYSE's 4th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) is strategically positioned as a powerhouse in the energy sector, leveraging a diversified business model focused on regulated utility rate increase dynamics and robust investments in energy infrastructure. As a key player, Sempra's comprehensive base rate approval, specifically the Oncor base rate review approval, strengthens its influence in the Texas electric utility base rate landscape. The company's operations, encompassing electric transmission and distribution services and natural gas pipelines and storage, showcase its commitment to energy infrastructure growth. Significant grid modernization at Oncor, paired with reliable utility capital expenditures (capex), underscores Sempra's proactive approach in enhancing transmission and distribution investment. Additionally, renewable energy projects Sempra, along with battery storage projects Sempra, highlight its dedication to sustainable energy solutions. With competitive strengths in Sempra regulated utilities (SoCalGas SDG&E Oncor) and a focus on regulated rate base expansion, Sempra offers promising avenues for Sempra investor earnings growth amid regulatory approvals and utility revenue requirement approval.

SECTION 3 RECENT NEWS, PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES & PAST MONTH ECONOMIC NEWS

3.1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 258 words

Tue May 5, 2026: Sempra Strengthens Board with Jennifer M. Kirk Appointment

Sempra has announced the appointment of Jennifer M. Kirk to its board of directors, effective 20th June 2024. This strategic move comes as the company aims to enhance its governance and oversight, particularly in financial disciplines and risk management.

Kirk, who currently serves as the global controller and chief accounting officer at Medtronic plc, brings over two decades of experience in the energy sector. Her extensive background in finance and capital market activities will be instrumental in guiding the company's future strategies. The announcement was made on 6th May 2026 at 18:23:07.706818, highlighting the company's commitment to board refreshment and diversity.

Jeffrey W. Martin, the chairman and CEO, emphasized the importance of diverse perspectives in the boardroom, stating that Kirk's accounting and energy expertise will greatly benefit the company as it strives to solidify its position as North America's premier energy infrastructure provider. Kirk will also serve on the audit committee and the safety, sustainability, and technology committee.

Before her tenure at Medtronic, Kirk spent over 20 years at Occidental Petroleum Corporation, where she held various senior roles including senior vice president of integration and value capture. Her leadership experience in a major energy company aligns well with Sempra's objectives of operational excellence and sustainable practices.

With Kirk's appointment, Sempra's board now consists of ten directors, three of whom have been elected in the last five years, indicating a proactive approach to governance. The company continues to be recognized for its commitment to sustainability and high-performance culture, ensuring that it remains a leader in the energy infrastructure space.

Recent News 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 346 words

Sat Apr 18, 2026: Sempra Secures Regulatory Approval for Oncor's Rate Review

Sempra has announced a significant regulatory milestone as the Public Utility Commission of Texas (PUCT) approved a final order in Oncor Electric Delivery Company LLC's comprehensive base rate review proceeding. This approval, dated 20th April 2026, secures an annual revenue requirement of approximately $6.97 billion for Oncor, in which Sempra holds an 80.25% interest, marking an increase of about $560 million, or 8.7%, over Oncor's adjusted annualized revenues as outlined in the rate application.

The approved order includes a revised regulatory capital structure ratio of 56.5% debt to 43.5% equity, an authorized return on equity of 9.75%, and an authorized cost of debt of 4.94%. These figures indicate a positive shift from Oncor's previous authorized capital structure, which was 57.5% debt to 42.5% equity, along with a return on equity of 9.70% and a cost of debt of 4.39%. This change is expected to bolster Oncor's financial health, benefiting both Sempra and its investors.

Additionally, the order establishes a self-insurance reserve with an annual accrual amount in rates of $200 million for storm costs and other self-insured losses, a notable increase from the previous $122 million. A five-year amortization period for applicable regulatory assets and liabilities has also been set, excluding rate case expenses and year-end 2024 deferred system resiliency plan costs.

Oncor anticipates that this regulatory approval will have a positive impact on its future earnings, cash flow, and credit metrics. The company is permitted to surcharge the difference between the new billing rates and its current rates for the period from January 1, 2026, until the new rates take effect, which will be 45 days from the date of the final order. Oncor plans to file for this surcharge shortly after the new billing rates become effective, with recovery expected throughout 2026.

This development is a significant step forward for Sempra and Oncor, enhancing their financial stability and positioning them for future growth in the energy sector. Investors may want to evaluate the implications of this approval on Sempra's profitability and stock valuations, as understanding the business dynamics is crucial before making investment decisions.

3.2 PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

Story 1 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 71 words

Strategic Infrastructure Asset Monetization - $10 Billion KKR/CPP Investments Transaction

Sempra has agreed to sell a 45% non-controlling interest in Sempra Infrastructure Partners to a KKR-led consortium for $10 billion, valuing the subsidiary at approximately $31.7 billion. The deal, expected to close in mid-2026, allows Sempra to retain a 25% stake while deconsolidating the unit to improve its corporate credit profile. Proceeds will be received in stages through approximately 2033, eliminating planned equity issuances for 2025-2029 and funding U.S. utility investments.

Story 2 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 64 words

Port Arthur LNG Phase 2 Final Investment Decision - $14 Billion Expansion with Full Equity Financing

Sempra Infrastructure approved the $14 billion Port Arthur LNG Phase 2 expansion, adding two liquefaction trains and approximately 13 million tonnes per annum (MTPA) of capacity. A consortium led by Blackstone Credit & Insurance is providing $7 billion for a 49.9% minority equity stake, fully funding the equity requirement. Commercial operations are targeted for 2030-2031, with long-term offtake agreements already securing over 7.5 MTPA.

Story 3 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 67 words

Port Arthur LNG Phase 2 Regulatory Authorization - Non-FTA Export Permit Approval

The U.S. Department of Energy granted Port Arthur LNG Phase 2 a permit to export 13.5 MTPA to non-free trade agreement countries, a critical regulatory step for broadening the project's global market reach. This authorization, along with prior FERC approval, supports the project's commercial viability and aligns with U.S. energy security goals. The permit was the first non-FTA authorization issued following the DOE's recent public interest study.

Story 4 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 57 words

Multi-Party LNG Offtake Agreements Portfolio - JERA, ConocoPhillips, and EQT Anchor Subscriptions

Sempra Infrastructure signed 20-year sales and purchase agreements with JERA (1.5 MTPA), ConocoPhillips (4 MTPA), and EQT Corporation (2 MTPA) for Port Arthur LNG Phase 2. These agreements commit 7.5 MTPA of the project's capacity, validating global demand and strengthening strategic partnerships. The deals were contingent on the final investment decision, which was successfully achieved in September.

Story 5 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 60 words

Q3 2025 Financial Results - Strong Earnings Beat and Guidance Reaffirmation

Sempra reported strong Q3 2025 adjusted earnings of $1.11 per share, beating analyst estimates, and reaffirmed its full-year EPS guidance for 2025 and 2026. The company highlighted successful execution of value creation initiatives, including the Port Arthur Phase 2 FID and asset sales. Capital deployment remains robust, particularly at Oncor, which is seeing significant growth driven by data center interconnections.

Story 6 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 63 words

Oncor Base Rate Case and Texas Regulatory Progress - $834 Million Rate Increase Request

Oncor filed a base rate case requesting an $834 million increase to cover storm recovery costs, inflation, and higher interest rates. The request aims to align rates with economic realities and support a massive capital investment plan for grid reliability in growing Texas markets. A settlement agreement has been approved regarding surcharges if proceedings extend past January 2026, with hearings scheduled for November.

Story 7 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 62 words

Energia Costa Azul LNG Phase 1 - Advanced Construction Progress Targeting Spring 2026 Commercial Operations

Construction on the Energia Costa Azul LNG Phase 1 terminal is over 90% complete, with commercial operations targeted for spring 2026. Despite earlier delays and cost pressures, the project is advancing toward its DOE export deadline, supported by a $1.58 billion debt financing package. The facility will produce approximately 3 MTPA, serving Pacific Basin markets through long-term agreements with Mitsui and TotalEnergies.

Story 8 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 59 words

Sempra Sustainability Recognition - 14th Consecutive Dow Jones Sustainability Index Inclusion

Sempra was named to the Dow Jones Sustainability Index North America for the 14th consecutive year, recognizing its leadership in ESG performance, risk management, and community relations. The distinction places Sempra among the top 20% of large North American companies for sustainability practices. This recognition complements other accolades, highlighting the company's focus on responsible business practices and shareholder value.

Story 9 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 54 words

Cameron LNG FERC Approval - Five-Year Extension for Expansion Construction Timeline

FERC granted Sempra a five-year extension, until March 2033, to complete the Cameron LNG expansion project in Louisiana. The request followed changes in federal LNG export policy and provides necessary flexibility for project planning and financing. The extension supports continued development of the facility, which remains a key component of U.S. LNG export capacity.

Story 10 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 62 words

SDG&E Golden Pacific Powerlink Transmission Project - Critical 500-kV Grid Reinforcement Initiative

SDG&E announced plans to build the Golden Pacific Powerlink, a 500-kV transmission line identified by CAISO as essential for California's grid reliability and carbon goals. The project will connect Imperial Valley to San Diego/Orange Counties, with construction slated to begin in 2029 for a 2032 in-service date. Costs will be shared statewide, limiting the impact on local SDG&E customers to approximately 9%.

May 7, 2026: AEP's $78 Billion Capital Plan Validates Texas Hyperscale Thesis, Strengthening Sempra's Oncor Growth Outlook

According to industry reports, American Electric Power lifted its five-year capital plan to $78 billion from $72 billion on May 5, 2026, citing accelerating contracted data center demand through 2030. The $6 billion expansion funds new transmission projects in SPP ($1.6 billion) and PJM ($1.9 billion), plus new gas-fired generation in Indiana, while contracted load rose to 63 GW with nearly 90% tied to hyperscalers.

Operational metrics indicate that AEP Texas alone accounts for 41 GW of large-load commitments, supported by the upcoming rollout of Texas Senate Bill 6. The plan reinforces a structural shift across US investor-owned utilities, where regulated rate base is expanding sharply to serve hyperscale and AI-driven load. AEP also raised its long-term operating earnings CAGR target to greater than 9% through 2030 and identified over $10 billion in additional line-of-sight investments beyond the current plan.

Impact on Sempra: SRE

Following this news, Sempra faces validation of its Texas-centric infrastructure thesis as AEP highlights ERCOT's 41 GW of contracted load commitments and the upcoming rollout of Texas Senate Bill 6. The bill is expected to provide greater certainty around interconnection timing for new load growth, a development that benefits Sempra's Oncor subsidiary, the largest transmission and distribution utility in Texas by customer count. Oncor's role in delivering power to data center, industrial, and residential customers across north and west Texas places Sempra in direct economic alignment with AEP Texas's load growth, since both utilities depend on shared ERCOT transmission planning, generation interconnection queues, and supply chain capacity.

Sempra's California utilities and LNG infrastructure businesses also benefit from the same secular electrification and AI-driven demand trends. Strategically, AEP's disclosure provides Sempra additional regulatory and investor support for its own multi-year Texas-led capital expansion.

May 07 2026: Sempra Confirms 2026 EPS Guidance, Eyes SI Partners Deal Closure

Sempra, NYSE:SRE, has affirmed its adjusted earnings per share (EPS) guidance for the fiscal year 2026, projecting a range of $4.80 to $5.30. This announcement was made during a recent earnings call, reflecting a positive start to the year for the company. On the 7th of May 2026, Sempra's Chairman, President, and CEO Jeffery Martin, highlighted that the company deployed $3 billion of investment capital in the first quarter, keeping it on track to meet its annual targets.

During the call, Martin also provided updates on regulatory matters that could impact Sempra's financial performance. Oncor, a subsidiary of Sempra, received approval from the Public Utility Commission of Texas (PUCT) for a settlement related to its base rate review. This agreement includes a higher authorized equity layer of 43.5%, a return on equity of 9.75%, and an increased cost of debt set at 4.94%. Similarly, in California, Sempra’s San Diego Gas & Electric (SDG&E) filed an uncontested settlement with the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) that could increase its authorized base return on equity to 10.28%. These developments, pending final approvals, are expected to bolster Sempra's financial standing.

In terms of operational milestones, Sempra anticipates producing its first liquefied natural gas (LNG) next month, with substantial completion of its LNG facilities expected in the summer. This pivotal step will allow the company to begin recognizing LNG revenues from long-term contracted sales, with full commercial operations commencing shortly thereafter. Martin indicated that the company is aiming to close the transaction related to SI Partners in the second or third quarter of 2026, further enhancing its growth trajectory.

For the first quarter of 2026, Sempra reported GAAP earnings of $1.37 billion, or $1.58 per share. On an adjusted basis, earnings stood at $991 million, translating to $1.51 per share. Executive VP and CFO Karen Sedgwick reiterated the company's commitment to maintaining a long-term EPS growth rate of 7% to 9%, while affirming its guidance for 2027 EPS in the range of $5.10 to $5.70. This strategic focus positions Sempra to navigate the evolving energy landscape effectively.

As Sempra continues to make significant investments and pursue strategic acquisitions, the market will be watching closely for how these initiatives will translate into shareholder value. With the affirmation of its earnings guidance and the impending closure of the SI Partners deal, Sempra is poised for an impactful year ahead.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-RY53-RVFD-P0K6-00000-00OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP - INDUSTRY WATCH: SECTOR TRENDS AND PEER PERSPECTIVES2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:41:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKOCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP - INDUSTRY WATCH: SECTOR TRENDS AND PEER PERSPECTIVES

DASHBOARD: OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP

PermID5073147691Websitehttps://octopus.energy/IndustryRenewable Energy Supply & GenerationAddress"UK House, 5th floor, 164-182 Oxford Street London W1D 1NN England, United Kingdom"

ACTIVITIES:

Octopus Energy Group, founded in 2015 by Greg Jackson, is a renewable energy provider headquartered in London, UK. Employing over 3,500 people, it generates $5 billion annually, serving 7 million+ customers across 18 countries. Its Kraken platform optimizes energy usage. In 2024, Octopus expanded U.S. operations. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, innovation, and customer focus, with training in clean tech and AI. Competing with EDF, Octopus stands out for its flexible tariffs and smart grid tech. Recent initiatives include EV charging networks and net-zero commitments, aligning with green energy trends. Valued at $9 billion in 2023, Octopus drives the global energy transition.

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP [3 Press Releases]

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GENERATION TO INVEST $500 MILLION TO REMOVE POLLUTING CO₂ FROM THE ATMOSPHERE

Major US deal to help cut up to 50 million tonnes of CO₂ - equivalent to eliminating the annual greenhouse gas emissions of New York City

Carbon credits to be snapped up by Google, Meta and McKinsey

Pushes Octopus closer to its ambitious $2bn US investment target, with a significant proportion in California's clean tech scene

London / San Francisco, 30 April 2026 - Octopus Energy Generation, one of Europe's leading renewables investors, is ramping up efforts to slash CO₂ pollution at scale - inking a major US deal that will help remove up to 50 million tonnes of carbon dioxide from the air.

Octopus's fund management team is set to invest $500 million in afforestation and reforestation projects in the US developed by public benefit and climate technology company Living Carbon.

Source: Company Website

+ POWERED UP: OCTOPUS AND MUIRHOUSE SLASH ENERGY BILLS FOR SCOTTISH SOCIAL HOMES BY £200

Muirhouse becomes one of Scotland's first housing associations to roll out Octopus Energy's groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff

UK's first green tech energy tariff that rewards both social housing landlords and tenants

Slashes electricity bills for social housing tenants by around £200 a year

Helping green home upgrades deliver real returns for landlords

London/Edinburgh, 29th April 2026 - Octopus Energy, the UK's largest energy supplier, has partnered with Muirhouse Housing Association to bring its groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff to over 30 social homes in Scotland.

Source: Company Website

+ 1 MILLION CUSTOMERS FOR EVERY TENTACLE: OCTOPUS ENERGY SUPERCHARGES TO 8 MILLION CUSTOMERS IN THE UK

The UK's largest energy supplier announces hitting 8 million home energy customers, with 2500 new customers joining daily

Market leading support for customers now includes You Pay We Pay, OctoAssist fund and the extension of their electric blanket scheme for Winter 2026

The milestone coincides with a doubling in heat pump sales and a 0.75 rise in solar sales as households shift to trusting in home grown power

London, 24th April 2026 - Octopus Energy has surpassed 8 million customers in the UK, further cementing its position as the nation's largest energy supplier.

With 2500 customers switching to Octopus every day, the gap has widened since Octopus claimed the top spot in January 2025, the first time the market lead had changed hands since privatisation in 1986.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. [3 Press Releases]

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD.: GOOD NEWS AND BAD NEWS: OUR BREAKING THE LINK CAMPAIGN IS WORKING - BUT WE NEED TO GO FURTHER

Britain's energy market is broken. For years, we've been campaigning to "break the link" between the soaring cost of fossil gas and the price of the cheap, green electricity we generate right here in Britain.

Now, the message is finally hitting home in the corridors of Westminster.

There was a flurry of excitement in mid-April 2026 as the chancellor, Rachel Reeves, announced that the government was looking at ways to break the link between electricity and fossil gas prices.

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTALK REINTRODUCE BEAVERS TO EAST SUSSEX AFTER A 400-YEAR ABSENCE

We've got amazing news from Ecotalk this spring with the release of a family of (extremely cute!) beavers into Bowyers Wood, East Sussex.

Chris Packham, Ecotalk's Chief Ecologist, was there to help the beavers get settled in. He said: "I just can't remember when I was last so excited about meeting a rodent! Big, fantastic rodents! What a fantastic day for Bowyers. And massive thanks to all our Ecotalk customers for making this possible."

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. OUR DAY WITH THE YOUNG GREEN BRITON CHALLENGE

Four members of the Ecotricity team recently took the hot seats as the judges at Stroud High School for entries to the Young Green Briton Challenge: a national programme that transforms students' climate ideas into real-world action.

For the 'Eco Dragon's Den', our team drew on their real-world expertise in green energy and sustainability. They used this to guide and challenge students on the impact and feasibility of their projects, offering constructive feedback to help sharpen each idea.

Source: Company Website

NYSE END-OF-WEEK STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

Sempra (NYSE:SRE $91.57), the NYSE's 4th largest Utility company by market cap, has dropped $3.55 (or 3.7%) in the past week to close at $91.57. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 128.1 points (or 1.8%) in the week, this represented a relative price change of -5.5%.

The shares had their worst week in three weeks since the week-ended Apr 23, 2026.

During the week its volatility (highest price minus lowest price/lowest price) of 7.2% was 2 times its average weekly volatility of 4.4%.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $2.3 billion to $59.8 billion.

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) is an American energy infrastructure company specializing in the development and operation of energy networks. The company is engaged in the construction and management of electricity grids and natural gas pipelines, offering reliable energy delivery services through its extensive network. Sempra's strategic investments in renewable energy projects and innovative technology solutions highlight its commitment to sustainability. With a robust pipeline of projects focused on clean energy transition, Sempra leverages its large-scale ecosystem and technological leadership. Key competitive advantages include its expansive supply chain and ability to innovate in the energy sector.

Release Date: February 26, 2026

Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 December 2025: Sempra reports 357% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit

Sempra (NYSE:SRE), announced net profit of $352m for the quarter-ended 31 December 2025 [Q4/2025], up 357% from the previous quarter [Q3/2025] and down 47% from the year-earlier period [Q4/2024]. Earnings per share (EPS) were up 350% sequentially from 12.0c in Q3/2025 to 54.0c in Q4/2025.

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Dec 2025)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q4/2024], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was down 48.6%, Revenue was down 0.2% and Net Profit was down 47.1%.

Thursday's price drop

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) drops to three-month low

SAN DIEGO, Thursday, May 07 2026 - Sempra's (NYSE: SRE $91.56) stock price traded at its 85-day low of $89.62. The last time the price hit that low level was on Wednesday, Feb 11, 2026. Sempra's stock price fell $2.11 (2.3%) from Wednesday's close of $91.56. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which fell 30.8 points (0.4%) in the day, the relative price change was -1.8%. There were 3,415,172 shares worth $312.7 million traded today; the volume was 0.9 times the average daily trading volume of 3.7 million shares.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

Sempra, a leading energy infrastructure company listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: SRE), is a Fortune 500 holding entity headquartered in San Diego, California. The company's core business revolves around the development, operation, and investment in energy infrastructure, including natural gas utilities, pipelines, storage facilities, and electric services across North America. Through subsidiaries like San Diego Gas & Electric and Southern California Gas Company, Sempra delivers reliable energy to millions of customers, emphasizing safety, sustainability, and operational efficiency. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects and liquefied natural gas (LNG) exports, driven by increasing demand for clean energy solutions and global market expansion. For instance, Sempra's investments in wind, solar, and battery storage, as well as its LNG facilities in Mexico and the U.S., have fueled significant growth, positioning the company as a key player in the energy transition. With a strong focus on innovation and strategic partnerships, Sempra continues to enhance its It is the NYSE's 4th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) is strategically positioned as a powerhouse in the energy sector, leveraging a diversified business model focused on regulated utility rate increase dynamics and robust investments in energy infrastructure. As a key player, Sempra's comprehensive base rate approval, specifically the Oncor base rate review approval, strengthens its influence in the Texas electric utility base rate landscape. The company's operations, encompassing electric transmission and distribution services and natural gas pipelines and storage, showcase its commitment to energy infrastructure growth. Significant grid modernization at Oncor, paired with reliable utility capital expenditures (capex), underscores Sempra's proactive approach in enhancing transmission and distribution investment. Additionally, renewable energy projects Sempra, along with battery storage projects Sempra, highlight its dedication to sustainable energy solutions. With competitive strengths in Sempra regulated utilities (SoCalGas SDG&E Oncor) and a focus on regulated rate base expansion, Sempra offers promising avenues for Sempra investor earnings growth amid regulatory approvals and utility revenue requirement approval.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

SAN DIEGO, Thursday, May 07 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Sempra has shown some weakness in recent days. Sempra (NYSE:SRE), the NYSE's 4th largest Utility company by market cap, fell 2.2% during its previous trading session to close at $91.57.

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) is an American energy infrastructure company specializing in the development and operation of energy networks. The company is engaged in the construction and management of electricity grids and natural gas pipelines, offering reliable energy delivery services through its extensive network. Sempra's strategic investments in renewable energy projects and innovative technology solutions highlight its commitment to sustainability. With a robust pipeline of projects focused on clean energy transition, Sempra leverages its large-scale ecosystem and technological leadership. Key competitive advantages include its expansive supply chain and ability to innovate in the energy sector.

SECTION 1 RECENT NEWS

Tue May 5, 2026: Sempra Strengthens Board with Jennifer M. Kirk Appointment

Sempra has announced the appointment of Jennifer M. Kirk to its board of directors, effective 20th June 2024. This strategic move comes as the company aims to enhance its governance and oversight, particularly in financial disciplines and risk management.

Kirk, who currently serves as the global controller and chief accounting officer at Medtronic plc, brings over two decades of experience in the energy sector. Her extensive background in finance and capital market activities will be instrumental in guiding the company's future strategies. The announcement was made on 6th May 2026 at 18:23:07.706818, highlighting the company's commitment to board refreshment and diversity.

Jeffrey W. Martin, the chairman and CEO, emphasized the importance of diverse perspectives in the boardroom, stating that Kirk's accounting and energy expertise will greatly benefit the company as it strives to solidify its position as North America's premier energy infrastructure provider. Kirk will also serve on the audit committee and the safety, sustainability, and technology committee.

Before her tenure at Medtronic, Kirk spent over 20 years at Occidental Petroleum Corporation, where she held various senior roles including senior vice president of integration and value capture. Her leadership experience in a major energy company aligns well with Sempra's objectives of operational excellence and sustainable practices.

With Kirk's appointment, Sempra's board now consists of ten directors, three of whom have been elected in the last five years, indicating a proactive approach to governance. The company continues to be recognized for its commitment to sustainability and high-performance culture, ensuring that it remains a leader in the energy infrastructure space.

Recent News 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 346 words

Sat Apr 18, 2026: Sempra Secures Regulatory Approval for Oncor's Rate Review

Sempra has announced a significant regulatory milestone as the Public Utility Commission of Texas (PUCT) approved a final order in Oncor Electric Delivery Company LLC's comprehensive base rate review proceeding. This approval, dated 20th April 2026, secures an annual revenue requirement of approximately $6.97 billion for Oncor, in which Sempra holds an 80.25% interest, marking an increase of about $560 million, or 8.7%, over Oncor's adjusted annualized revenues as outlined in the rate application.

The approved order includes a revised regulatory capital structure ratio of 56.5% debt to 43.5% equity, an authorized return on equity of 9.75%, and an authorized cost of debt of 4.94%. These figures indicate a positive shift from Oncor's previous authorized capital structure, which was 57.5% debt to 42.5% equity, along with a return on equity of 9.70% and a cost of debt of 4.39%. This change is expected to bolster Oncor's financial health, benefiting both Sempra and its investors.

Additionally, the order establishes a self-insurance reserve with an annual accrual amount in rates of $200 million for storm costs and other self-insured losses, a notable increase from the previous $122 million. A five-year amortization period for applicable regulatory assets and liabilities has also been set, excluding rate case expenses and year-end 2024 deferred system resiliency plan costs.

Oncor anticipates that this regulatory approval will have a positive impact on its future earnings, cash flow, and credit metrics. The company is permitted to surcharge the difference between the new billing rates and its current rates for the period from January 1, 2026, until the new rates take effect, which will be 45 days from the date of the final order. Oncor plans to file for this surcharge shortly after the new billing rates become effective, with recovery expected throughout 2026.

This development is a significant step forward for Sempra and Oncor, enhancing their financial stability and positioning them for future growth in the energy sector. Investors may want to evaluate the implications of this approval on Sempra's profitability and stock valuations, as understanding the business dynamics is crucial before making investment decisions.

SECTION 5 CORPORATE PROFILE

5.1 Activities

Sempra, a leading energy infrastructure company listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: SRE), is a Fortune 500 holding entity headquartered in San Diego, California. The company's core business revolves around the development, operation, and investment in energy infrastructure, including natural gas utilities, pipelines, storage facilities, and electric services across North America. Through subsidiaries like San Diego Gas & Electric and Southern California Gas Company, Sempra delivers reliable energy to millions of customers, emphasizing safety, sustainability, and operational efficiency. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects and liquefied natural gas (LNG) exports, driven by increasing demand for clean energy solutions and global market expansion. For instance, Sempra's investments in wind, solar, and battery storage, as well as its LNG facilities in Mexico and the U.S., have fueled significant growth, positioning the company as a key player in the energy transition. With a strong focus on innovation and strategic partnerships, Sempra continues to enhance its It is the NYSE's 4th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) is strategically positioned as a powerhouse in the energy sector, leveraging a diversified business model focused on regulated utility rate increase dynamics and robust investments in energy infrastructure. As a key player, Sempra's comprehensive base rate approval, specifically the Oncor base rate review approval, strengthens its influence in the Texas electric utility base rate landscape. The company's operations, encompassing electric transmission and distribution services and natural gas pipelines and storage, showcase its commitment to energy infrastructure growth. Significant grid modernization at Oncor, paired with reliable utility capital expenditures (capex), underscores Sempra's proactive approach in enhancing transmission and distribution investment. Additionally, renewable energy projects Sempra, along with battery storage projects Sempra, highlight its dedication to sustainable energy solutions. With competitive strengths in Sempra regulated utilities (SoCalGas SDG&E Oncor) and a focus on regulated rate base expansion, Sempra offers promising avenues for Sempra investor earnings growth amid regulatory approvals and utility revenue requirement approval.
